audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for YHWH covenant reassurance, fourfold liberation verbs, genealogy of Moses/Aaron, and renewed commission.

verses.restored: YHWH said to Moses, Now you shall see what I will do to Pharaoh; for by strong hand he will send them, and by strong hand he will drive them from his land.
Divine spoke to Moses and said to him, I am YHWH.
I appeared to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob as El Shaddai, but by my name YHWH I was not known to them.
And also I established my covenant with them, to give them land of Canaan, land of their sojournings in which they sojourned.
And also I have heard the groaning of the children of Israel, whom Egypt is making serve, and I remembered my covenant.

Therefore say to the children of Israel: I am YHWH, and I will bring you out from under burdens of Egypt, and I will rescue you from their service, and I will redeem you with outstretched arm and with great judgments.
I will take you to myself as people, and I will be to you as Divine; and you shall know that I am YHWH your Divine, the One bringing you out from under burdens of Egypt.
I will bring you into the land that I lifted my hand to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and I will give it to you as possession. I am YHWH.
Moses spoke thus to the children of Israel, but they did not listen to Moses because of shortness of spirit/breath and hard service.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Come, speak to Pharaoh king of Egypt, that he send the children of Israel from his land.
Moses spoke before YHWH, saying, Look, the children of Israel did not listen to me; how then will Pharaoh listen to me, and I am uncircumcised of lips?
YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron and commanded them concerning the children of Israel and concerning Pharaoh king of Egypt, to bring the children of Israel out from land of Egypt.
These are heads of their fathers' houses: sons of Reuben firstborn of Israel: Hanoch and Pallu, Hezron and Carmi; these are clans of Reuben.
Sons of Simeon: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul son of a Canaanite woman; these are clans of Simeon.
These are names of sons of Levi according to their generations: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari; years of life of Levi were one hundred thirty-seven years.
Sons of Gershon: Libni and Shimei, according to their clans.
Sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel; years of life of Kohath were one hundred thirty-three years.
Sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. These are clans of Levi according to their generations.
Amram took Jochebed his father's sister for himself as wife, and she bore to him Aaron and Moses; years of life of Amram were one hundred thirty-seven years.
Sons of Izhar: Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
Sons of Uzziel: Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
Aaron took Elisheba daughter of Amminadab, sister of Nahshon, for himself as wife, and she bore to him Nadab and Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
Sons of Korah: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph; these are clans of Korahites.
Eleazar son of Aaron took for himself one of daughters of Putiel as wife, and she bore to him Phinehas. These are heads of fathers of Levites according to their clans.
This is Aaron and Moses to whom YHWH said, Bring out the children of Israel from land of Egypt according to their hosts.
These are the ones speaking to Pharaoh king of Egypt to bring out the children of Israel from Egypt; this is Moses and Aaron.
It happened on day YHWH spoke to Moses in land of Egypt,
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying, I am YHWH. Speak to Pharaoh king of Egypt all that I speak to you.
Moses said before YHWH, Look, I am uncircumcised of lips; how will Pharaoh listen to me?

translation_notes: YHWH identity is tied to liberation verbs: bring out, rescue, redeem, take as people, be Divine.
Shortness of spirit/breath explains why oppressed people cannot yet receive promise; this is trauma-aware, not blame.
Uncircumcised of lips extends Moses' speech objection; preserve without shaming.
Genealogy interrupts narrative to root Moses/Aaron in Levi and named kinship.
safety_note: Do not blame Israel for not listening; the text gives the reason: crushed breath/spirit and hard service. Trauma and exhaustion impede reception.
